lessons.title: Examinership
lessons.description: This is the process by which a company in financial difficulty can be given breathing space, protected from its creditors, and given a genuine chance to survive.

lessons.title: Receivership
lessons.description: In the last lesson, we looked at examinership — a process designed to rescue a company. Receivership is something quite different. A receiver is not there to save the company.

lessons.title: Liquidation, or Winding Up
lessons.description: This is the process by which a company's assets are realised, its debts are paid, and the company is dissolved. It is the end of the road.

lessons.title: Realisation of Corporate Assets — Voidable Transactions
lessons.description: In the previous lessons, we looked at examinership, receivership, and liquidation.
